conditions: intro phase lasts 2 weeks; avoid interpreting early underperformance as stall

rationale_templates.increase_load: Performance exceeded target range. Increase load next exposure.
rationale_templates.hold_load: Performance stayed in range. Hold load and chase the rep ceiling.
rationale_templates.reduce_load: Performance repeatedly fell below target. Reduce load to restore quality.
rationale_templates.deload: Fatigue and underperformance indicate that a short deload is warranted.

source_sections.heading: Important Program Notes
source_sections.excerpt: For the first 2 weeks of this Full Body version of the program, most sets are taken to an RPE of ~7-8 or ~8-9. This means you will be leaving 1-3 reps in the tank on most exercises.

source_sections.heading: Important Program Notes
source_sections.excerpt: For the first 2 weeks of this Full Body version of the program, most sets are taken to an RPE of ~7-8 or ~8-9. This means you will be leaving 1-3 reps in the tank on most exercises.

source_sections.heading: Important Program Notes
source_sections.excerpt: All other aspects of the program, including how to understand the Last-Set Intensity Technique column, when to make an exercise substitution and how to progress through the rep ranges given is explained in The Hypertrophy Handbook.

source_sections.heading: Important Program Notes
source_sections.excerpt: This only lasts for the first two weeks to get you adapted to the higher training frequency. For these first two weeks, there are also fewer intensity techniques used. After the first two weeks, the intensity will increase and most sets will be taken to an RPE of 9-10.

source_sections.heading: Workout Table Columns
source_sections.excerpt: Exercise Last-Set Intensity Technique Substitution Option 1 Substitution Option 2 Notes
